### Post by vinnie107 on 2009-05-23
Hi, im having touble surfing the internet with ubuntu.

I can connect ok but when i type an address i dont get anything. It will just say "looking up 'website'" at the bottom.

I presume this is a DNS problem.

Anyone have any ideas?

---

### Post by Brandon Williams on 2009-05-23
First, check the content of /etc/resolv.conf and make sure that the specified nameserver looks OK. On my system, it looks like this:
```
$ cat /etc/resolv.conf
# Generated by NetworkManager
domain local
search local
nameserver 192.168.1.1
```
The nameserver specified here is my internet gateway, which handles both dhcp and dns for my network. If you're using dhcp, then you should be getting the setting from your dhcp server.

To verify that the nameserver setting is good, run dig from the command line, as in:
```
$ dig www.google.com
; <<>> DiG 9.5.1-P2 <<>> www.google.com
;; global options:  printcmd
;; Got answer:
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 33732
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 5, AUTHORITY: 0, ADDITIONAL: 0

;; QUESTION SECTION:
;www.google.com.			IN	A

;; ANSWER SECTION:
www.google.com.		573654	IN	CNAME	www.l.google.com.
www.l.google.com.	1	IN	A	66.102.1.104
www.l.google.com.	1	IN	A	66.102.1.99
www.l.google.com.	1	IN	A	66.102.1.147
www.l.google.com.	1	IN	A	66.102.1.103

;; Query time: 11 msec
;; SERVER: 192.168.1.1#53(192.168.1.1)
;; WHEN: Sat May 23 13:09:10 2009
;; MSG SIZE  rcvd: 126
```

If you don't see an ANSWER SECTION or the output indicates an error of some kind, then you know that there's a core name resolution issue. If you do receive addresses when using dig but have trouble in your browser with the same name lookup, then we'll have to look further.

---

### Post by Iowan on 2009-05-24

### Post by Kareeser on 2009-05-24
If your network is automatically configured by Ubuntu, then your DNS server should be your local gateway. If it works with Vista, then the router is fine. You can successfully ping websites, correct?

Then a DNS problem on your computer is not the problem... what **IS**, I have no clue yet...

### Post by superprash2003 on 2009-05-26
does [http://209.85.171.100](http://209.85.171.100) open immediately?? if so , its a DNS Issue , use opendns servers -208.67.222.222 and 208.67.220.220

for reference : [http://www.prash-babu.com/2008/04/how-to-configure-dns-servers-in-linux.html](http://www.prash-babu.com/2008/04/how-to-configure-dns-servers-in-linux.html)

### Post by dmizer on 2009-05-26
> **vinnie107 said:**
> I tried that DNS thing it worked for a little while then stopped.

Thats the thing with this, its very on and off.
